% Post-#60-Path-C manuscript snippet (from manuscript repo 35c964a onwards)
% This file mirrors rollback_60_real.tex but with Path C conditional-framework
% wording. Used as F2 fixture in TestR4PatternAFrameworkAware: validates that
% R4 PATTERN-A does NOT false-positive when axiom prop frames boundary as
% conditional / locally-invoked (post-Path-C state).

\section{Setup (excerpt — conditional boundary, post-Path-C)}
\label{sec:setup}

A sensitivity family $\{\xi_s\}_{s \in S}$ satisfies \emph{Iverson's law of
similarity} if there exist continuous functions $\gamma, \eta$ with
$\xi_s(\lambda x) = \xi_{\eta(\lambda,s)}(x)$ for $\lambda \in J$.
Following the framework of \citet{DobleHsu2020} and
\citet{GselmannDobleHsu2025}, boundary conditions (such as
$\gamma(1, s) = 1$, $\eta(1, s) = s$, or $H(0) = 0$) are imposed locally
in theorems that invoke them (e.g.\ Theorem~\ref{thm:mult-trans}), not as
part of the universal similarity definition.

\section{Theorem 1 (excerpt — Path C f-extension hypothesis)}
\label{sec:thm1}

\begin{theorem}[Gain-control with $\eta(\lambda,s)=f(s)$]
\label{thm:eta-s}
Suppose $\xi$ satisfies Iverson's law of similarity with
$\eta(\lambda, s) = f(s)$ for a continuous function $f\colon S \to S$
(the $\eta = s$ specialization corresponds to $f = \mathrm{id}_S$, subsumed
in what follows).
\end{theorem}

\textbf{Case (B): Logarithmic.}
$u(y) = a \ln y + d + e\,c$,
$v(x) = a b \ln x + d$, $\sigma(x) = c$ const.,
$\gamma(\lambda, s) = \lambda^b\, e^{c(s - f(s))/a}$
(reduces to $\lambda^b$ at $f = \mathrm{id}$; $f$ may be any continuous map here).

\textbf{Case (C): Power.}
$u(y) = c\,e\, y^a + d$,
$\gamma(\lambda, s) = \lambda^b\, \sqrt[a]{(s + c_2/c)/(f(s) + c_2/c)}$
(reduces to $\lambda^b$ at $f = \mathrm{id}$; $f$ may be any continuous map here).
